Class VaultEncryptingStrategy

java.lang.Object
com.seq.acheron.secrets.symmetric.VaultEncryptingStrategy
Direct Known Subclasses:
AESVaultEncryptingStrategy, PBKDF2VaultEncryptingStrategy
public abstract class VaultEncryptingStrategy extends Object
Abstract base class for symmetric encryption strategies used by the vault.

This class models the common pattern used in password managers:

  • A derivedKey obtained from the user's master password via a KDF (e.g., Argon2, PBKDF2).
  • A randomly generated vaultKey which is used to encrypt and decrypt the actual vault data (typically with AES-GCM).
Concrete subclasses are responsible for initializing derivedKey in their constructors.

  • Field Details

    • derivedKey

      protected SecretKey derivedKey
      Key derived from the user's master password via a KDF.

      This key is typically used to encrypt and decrypt the vaultKey, not the vault contents directly.

    • vaultKey

      protected SecretKey vaultKey
      Symmetric key used to encrypt and decrypt vault data.

      This key may be generated randomly in the constructor or assigned later by calling importVaultKey(String).

    • transformation

      protected final String transformation
      JCE transformation string, e.g. "AES/GCM/NoPadding".

  • Constructor Details

    • VaultEncryptingStrategy

      protected VaultEncryptingStrategy(String transformation, boolean generateVaultKey) throws GeneralSecurityException
      Creates a new encrypting strategy and optionally generates a random vaultKey.

      Subclasses are expected to initialize derivedKey in their constructors, typically by applying a KDF to the master password.

      Parameters:
      transformation - the cipher transformation, e.g. "AES/GCM/NoPadding"
      generateVaultKey - if true, a new random vaultKey will be generated; if false, the vaultKey remains null until importVaultKey(String) is called
      Throws:
      GeneralSecurityException - if key generation fails

    • VaultEncryptingStrategy

      protected VaultEncryptingStrategy(String transformation, SecretKey vaultKey)
      Creates a new encrypting strategy using an existing vaultKey.

      This constructor is intended for reopening an existing vault where the vault key has already been unwrapped using the derivedKey.

      Parameters:
      transformation - the cipher transformation
      vaultKey - an existing vault key to reuse

  • Method Details

    • exportVaultKey

      public String exportVaultKey() throws GeneralSecurityException
      Exports the current vaultKey in encrypted form using the derivedKey.

      The raw vault key bytes are first encoded as Base64, and then encrypted with AES-GCM using the derivedKey. The return value is a Base64 string containing IV || ciphertext.

      Returns:
      Base64-encoded, encrypted representation of the vault key
      Throws:
      GeneralSecurityException - if encryption fails or derivedKey is not initialized

    • importVaultKey

      public SecretKey importVaultKey(String encryptedVaultKeyBase64) throws GeneralSecurityException
      Imports (unwraps) a vault key previously exported by exportVaultKey() using the current derivedKey.

      This method both reconstructs the key and assigns it to vaultKey, and also returns it so callers can reuse the instance or pass the key elsewhere.

      Parameters:
      encryptedVaultKeyBase64 - Base64-encoded encrypted vault key as produced by exportVaultKey()
      Returns:
      a SecretKey instance representing the original vault key
      Throws:
      GeneralSecurityException - if decryption fails or the input is malformed

    • encrypt

      public String encrypt(String plainText) throws GeneralSecurityException
      Encrypts the given plain-text using the current vaultKey.

      This is the primary method used to encrypt vault data.

      Parameters:
      plainText - the plain-text to encrypt
      Returns:
      Base64-encoded IV || ciphertext
      Throws:
      GeneralSecurityException - if encryption fails

    • decrypt

      public String decrypt(String ivAndCiphertextBase64) throws GeneralSecurityException
      Decrypts a Base64-encoded IV || ciphertext string using the current vaultKey.

      This is the primary method used to decrypt vault data.

      Parameters:
      ivAndCiphertextBase64 - Base64-encoded IV || ciphertext
      Returns:
      the decrypted plain-text string
      Throws:
      GeneralSecurityException - if decryption fails
